evals.prompt: Build a full-stack feature for managing team members. Each member has a name, email, role (admin/member/viewer), and active status. Need CRUD on backend and a list/create UI on frontend.
evals.expected_output: Coordinated backend API and frontend feature module with types, service, hooks, and components
expectations: Backend follows api-endpoint skill pattern: schema + service + router + test
Frontend types at features/team-members/types/ match backend response shape
Frontend service uses apiClient from @/shared/lib/api-client, not raw fetch
Frontend hooks use AbortController for cleanup in useEffect
Frontend uses logger from @/shared/lib/logger, not console.log
Feature has an index.ts that exports public API
Backend and frontend types are consistent (same field names, matching nullability)
Loading states are handled in components

evals.prompt: Create a notifications feature. Backend: store notifications per user with read/unread status. Frontend: show notification list with mark-as-read functionality.
evals.expected_output: Full-stack notification system with backend persistence and frontend reactivity
expectations: Backend service has mark_as_read method that updates notification status
Backend uses soft delete pattern, not hard delete
Frontend has separate hooks for list, mutations, and single notification
Frontend service file is in features/notifications/services/
Backend router uses Annotated dependency injection pattern
